description: Square has been very positive for us. We appreciate the safety/security of a large name, trusted brand, without the high cost that can often come along with that name. The cost for their equipment is reasonable. They are so frequently used that finding support is very easy as everyone I know uses them.
author: Lake M.
pros: Square has been a powerful platform for our nonprofit. We are able to process payments with a premium feel product, without the ongoing costs that many other POS systems have. The pay-as-we go type structure is flexible for our nonprofit. We do not bill frequently enough to justify a monthly fee, and so this option has been incredible. It was super easy to make our own, customizing the products, prices, receipts, and options at the click of a button. It integrates powerfully with other tools that use. This makes sales online, over the phone, through invoice, or on-site at events a breeze, and the platform is easy enough that anyone on my team can use it with their user/team options.
cons: We have faced some challenges when out on the site with connectivity of card readers, particularly those that connect via Bluetooth to an iPad. Once connected they are quick and easy to use, however the initial connection during a period can be tough. We are an infrequent use organization, since we accept payments with this platform during events, it could be months without using and I think this contributes to the difficulty versus it connecting to the device every day. I do find the iPad layout to be a bit more clunky, feeling like an enlarged iPhone layout versus its own dedicated design.

description: It was great starting out, but proved that it lacked the complexity to grow as my business grew. The low cost and user-friendly nature of the site is ideal when starting out.
author: Jocelyn W.
pros: The website builder was easy to navigate and the analytical were also very helpful. The square online analytics were about to pin point the time, location and funnel my website traffic was coming from
cons: The website was very limited in its build. It was difficult to embed widgets and other code that was is produced by a third party such as a booking page, google analytics code etc. Example: Square does not allow certain pages to have sub drop down pages.

review_title: Not ready for any online store other than simple boutiques or resturants.
description: Very poor. Even though I'm an admin on our account, for two years Square has not been able to verify me when I contact them for support. Even our store [sensitive content hidden] can't get help when he calls.
author: John C.
pros: The design is intriguing. The website is driven directly from the POS data so there are no separate "product records". This makes inventory sync automatic but the backbone of the site is subject to the quality of the POS data.
cons: Too many things to list fully. There is no reporting or management capability of images except one-at-a-time. For large image repositories, this is death. There is no built in under-construction concept so you have to build a fake page and then disable navigation to work on a dev site. To preview it, you have to turn on nav and preview while editing a live site. Large data sets are hopeless with their tools. Support is terrible. You get passed from department to department and often they can't verify your profile to help you. There is no way to build a content page that isn't a product record. Overall the development tools are not ready for prime time. Square sells you on using variations... beware. They have tons of limitations.

description: Easy to set up the payment system with card readers and then link it to the free website. Good support. Great payment facility - our members far prefer to pay online in advance or by card on the spot
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like the joined-up card reader and online payment facilities, especially with free website. By paying a bit more our members can login and check their past and coming orders.
cons: Inability to accept bookings for later payment (online or via card reader) for things like club events

author: Joe F.
pros: Seamless integration to POS inventory and generally square customer list. Constant improvement in design options.
cons: Inability to be more flexible with local delivery. Inability to set shipping/delivery at the item variation level. Clunky transactional email set up process. Stories function isn’t smooth when setting up new stories.

review_title: Square Online - A great system for small businesses
description: Very good, I would highly recommend it. Some improvements required but then like most systems its a continual evolution.
author: Richie P.
pros: Ease of setup and close integration with other Square Products
cons: Its lost a lot of flexibility in terms of design, but this is improving. Also there is not a lot of basic marketing functionality easily available like customer acquisition tools, cross selling and upselling functionality.

review_title: Easy set-up and easier to use
description: Customer service was quick to respond and very helpful in answering our multiple questions about using the service. The first time we used it, it went seamlessly. Have to say it continues to be seamless still.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: As selling on-line was new to us, the ease of set-up was very welcome. Information on the website was extremely helpful in assisting to make an informed decision to use this service.
cons: Nothing comes to mind currently as we have only used it for a very short time. (couple months). Since we are new to this e-commerce process, I am thinking there may come a time when it will get tedious or frustrating. Not sure. :)
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: This was nice and helpful to be able to add online orders to our business
author: Sadie S.
pros: Pretty easy to use. Love having my library easily integrated
cons: Could be a little more customizable for businesses. Some features are on square online but not other parts of square - they are good features but I would like to be able to adjust them in other places too (like the order of how modifiers show up on the register). Also - this one is huge - there really needs to be a better way to make sure orders are seen/accepted by employees! We ended up adding a motion sensor to the top so that it makes a sound loud enough when an order comes in.

review_title: Wonderful Platform For All Things Financial
description: Overall ive had a good experience. No real complaints. I would 100% recommend using it for your business transactions
author: Carlie R.
pros: Easy use, customer first model, well made interface that allows for money management to be stress free
cons: The only complaint i have with square online is the speed in which customer service requests are handled.

review_title: Easy for non-programmers to integrate into websites
description: Easy for a non-programmer to integrate into a simple website. I didn’t run into any problems and neither did my customers.
author: Christine B.
pros: Very easy to integrate with my Wordpress website. Surprisingly, The links all worked on my very first attempt.
cons: Square charges merchant processing fees, so they take a cut of every transaction which is never fun. Shop around - fee vary widely.

review_title: I’ll never go back to a “typical” credit card processor again
description: During this time where people are still a little wary of dining in restaurants, square online has been a vital tool in helping me serve customers through delivery and pickup.
author: Chris D.
pros: I love almost everything about square. The way it catalogs all of my merchandise to the ease of access for customer info, do building my website to managing payroll and the loyalty program that engages my customers and makes shopping with me more fun for them.
cons: There really isn’t much I can think of here, square really packs a punch but they could add a “processing fee” option where we can pass the credit card processing percentage on to the customer and remove it as a “cash incentive”

description: Functionality of the website and operation is excellent. Customer service and knowledge of staff is not what it should be and information regarding fees is unclear.
author: Heather T.
pros: I loved how easy everything was and that it was very similar to the way Apple products work.
cons: There are a LOT of hidden costs with Square. We were going to use Square for our entire operation as it has features for a food business that no one else in the market has. Unfortunately Square charge 50p per online order that is not explained clearly on their website. As we process thousands of orders per week that cost is huge compared to rivals. Square were unable to provide an explanation of what that 50p per order was for and as it can’t be justified we cannot transfer our complete customer base to Square.

author: Bridgette K.
pros: What I like most about Square online is it is beyond user friendly for me and my customers. It is a widely accepted software and my customers were able to pay simply by clicking on a link or invoice. Square also has donation links. This came in handy when I was raising money. Once I am paid, Square offers the option to be paid instantly or later that day via their card and checking account feature.
cons: There was a bit of delay with payments in order to verify my identity. Once I submitted all required documents, funds were released.

description: This product is very easy to figure out and navigate.
author: Brandi H.
pros: It's easy to navigate. And it makes ordering a breeze.
cons: I haven't found anything negative about Square

description: for our company is really usefull and very good app
author: alex M.
pros: we use square for our company it give us facility
cons: the rate to transfer money sometimes is a lot

author: Natasha L.
pros: Square Online is simple to use and track purchases.
cons: I have not had issues using Square Online.

description: It was a positive experience but I ended up choosing another provider.
author: Zach D.
pros: It was easy to charge people for my camps.
cons: Sometimes the fees were a little steep for my liking.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Amber A.
pros: I’ve lived using square products. They’re very easy to navigate and set up
cons: Sometimes I wish the mobile app had all the same features as the web but doesn’t hinder me using it

review_title: Excellent Software for a Small Business
author: Thea L.
pros: I love that Square Online allows for inventory integration across the website and the app. I sell both online and in-person and it is critical that the software removes sold items from the site automatically if I get an in-person sale.
cons: The website editor is a bit clunky. It is hard to find item listings previously listed, even when doing a search for the exact item name. I also have problems when trying to add inventory numbers to the item listing. I need to save and refresh several times before I can add this information.

author: Karl S.
pros: The software was very easy to setup. I was able to configure a website, add items to sell, and get up and running quickly.
cons: Inventory management is a mess if you want to provide bundles or bulk discounts automatically. Square Online has coupons, but the user must apply them. There is no way to provide bulk discounts or bundled discounts. Manually creating such bundles is possible, but adds to inventory complexity. My Square inventory never matches my real counts because I have to manually adjust for bundles.

description: The overall experience with Square Online had been good. I really enjoy using it, not too difficult to use. Square gives me a lot of data and charts that I use at meeting to do an overall performance of the store.
author: Colleen M.
pros: Online ordering and inventory are most impact feature.
cons: The feature that I feel could be improved are set up, it was hard to understand how to add items. Variation was hard to set up. An explanation that could pop up when you click over it would be helpful to understand what the feature is used for. The feature page online I would like to be able to manipulate more to make it my own, would like to see more options in this area like adjusting logos that I uploaded and being able to move the boxes that you click on to use.

review_title: All Square, but will Shop-If-I have a need/opportunity to try a different provider 😉
description: Overall, each individual component part is quite strong. And several of them do work quite well together when aligned with the more common use cases (e.g. accepting bookings for a hairstylist followed by in-person payments, or estimates and invoice management for a service business). However, as you start layering in additional complexity or are looking at less common needs/applications, the simplicity that is arguably its core selling feature in the beginning ends up working against it as you start to feel like you've got "lots of different things, in lots of different places, and some of it is repetitive but isn't always, and some of it syncs automatically but sometimes not, and........" well... you get the drift.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Overall, they offer a robust service suite with sleek POS tools, a clean end-user UI, strong inventory management, and offer a strong, trusted brand name in the market.
cons: I get the impression Square is best suited for rather "single-dimensional" businesses -- when you start to get into more dynamic and multi-faceted distribution channels, order types, variable inventory and order process flows, etc., it starts to feel a bit clunky.

review_title: A thoughtfully-designed and feature-packed site builder that is painless to navigate!
description: I'm really satisfied with how simple my Square Online site was to create, and is to stylize and update. It looks fabulous, even with the robust free features, but it's a powerhouse with the analytics on the backend. I love that I can link my Square Online site as a subdomain to be able to use it as a booking website for my photography business. Cutting down on email communication with clients, which has Square Online has made possible through its Square Appointments integration with Square Online, has been an invaluable tool for prioritizing time in-person with my clients. Square Online looks great, it's functional, and it's easy to use. It's a primary tool in my business' toolkit.
author: Spencer I.
pros: For how feature-packed the product is, It's very user-friendly to navigate and set up. For how great the complete product looks, it shouldn't be so simple to use.
cons: Connecting my Square Online site a subdomain was not a simple task. Establishing the SSL certificate credentials with my web host took a little extra time.

description: We are a small nonprofit theatre. We use Square to sell tickets and process donatons.
author: Rebecca P.
pros: It's easy to use. We use the free version.
cons: I wish it had a ticket sales/box office report function. I also wish they had a better fee structure for nonprofits.

review_title: Why you should go with Square
description: I love that I can post new products to my website and then tag them on my social media platforms all within 5 min. It just makes sooo many things simpler.
author: Tanya S.
pros: The ease of setting up my website was amazing!!! I had no website design experience prior to setting mine up. It was so easy. I just followed the prompts and read some of the tips and voilà! I have an amazing website!! I get compliments from my customers all time on how nice my website looks. They are absolutely amazed that I didn’t myself with no prior experience. If I can do this anyone can!!
cons: I can’t really think of anything that sticks out to me that I don’t like. The few issues that I’ve had were usually my error or not quite understanding what was needed. Just like with any computer software once you learn it and get familiar with it it’s a breeze.
